rulings.Case Title: MEERA BALAKUMAR VS. EMMA FAHY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 1, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT EMMA FAHY, BOBAN VELJANOV MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. The demurer is overruled as to the causes of action for negligence, invasion of privacy, Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress and nuisance. These causes of action are sufficiently stated. Demurrer to the cause of action for civil stalking is sustained with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of this cause of action. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ANAND KUMAR ET AL VS. ANKIT GARG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 1, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT ANKIT GARG, SONIA SAWKAR Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint is GRANTED and Defendant 66 Cleary Court Condominium Owners Association's request for sanctions is denied. California law strongly favors granting leave to amend absent legally cognizable prejudice to the opposing party. 66 Cleary has not shown that it would suffer any legally cognizable prejudice by having to defend the proposed cross-claims, which are based on the same set of facts as the complaint. 66 Cleary's arguments that the proposed cross-claims fail for lack of standing are better addressed on a pleading motion, not a motion to amend. 66 Cleary's motion for sanctions is based on the incorrect premise that the motion to amend was frivolous. It was not. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
